question_title: Will the interest in “netanyahu dead” change between 2026-03-16 and 2026-03-24 according to Google Trends?
summary:
The search term "netanyahu dead" spiked to 100 on Mar 15, 2026 driven by a viral conspiracy theory about an AI-generated video showing Netanyahu with 6 fingers. Netanyahu personally debunked it with a coffee shop video on Mar 15. The starting value on Mar 16 is 60, already declining 40% from peak.

The resolution compares Mar 16 (60) vs Mar 24 using a ±3 threshold, making the "Doesn't change" band extremely narrow [57-63]. Historical patterns show the previous spike (Mar 1-3, peaked at 13) returned to baseline (~1) within 3-4 days. Even with the ongoing Israel-Iran war providing a higher floor (~10-20), 8 days of decay from 60 should bring the value well below 57.

Monte Carlo simulations modeling post-spike decay with war-related spike risks give Decreases ~89% (blending conservative and aggressive models). The main upside risk comes from the active Israel-Iran war and IRGC threats against Netanyahu, which could trigger new death rumors, but for "Increases" the value would need to exceed 63 — requiring sustained extreme interest or a new event surpassing the already-debunked conspiracy peak.

factors.description: Starting value of 60 is extremely elevated, driven by a specific viral conspiracy (AI-generated video with 6 fingers). Netanyahu debunked it with a coffee video on Mar 15. The specific catalyst for the spike is resolved, strongly suggesting decay.
Historical decay pattern: First spike (Mar 1-3, peaked at 13) returned to baseline (~1) within 3-4 days. Post-spike decay is the dominant pattern for this search term.

The 'Doesn't change' band is extremely narrow: [57, 63]. Only a 7-point window out of 0-100. For a volatile, event-driven search term at an extreme level, landing in this narrow band 8 days later is very unlikely.

Active Israel-Iran war (started Feb 28, 2026) is ongoing. IRGC explicitly threatened to continue pursuing Netanyahu. Israeli military says war will last 3+ more weeks. New attacks or disappearances could re-trigger death rumors.
Monte Carlo simulations: Base model gives Decreases ~97%, aggressive model (higher spike probability, slower decay) gives Decreases ~78%. Blended estimate: Decreases ~89%.

For 'Increases' to occur, value must exceed 63 on Mar 24. This requires either sustained interest at current extreme level OR a new spike exceeding Mar 15's conspiracy-driven peak. The debunking reduces the conspiracy angle.

reasoning:
**Forecast: "netanyahu dead" Google Trends interest change (Mar 16–24)**

| Outcome | Probability |
|---------|------------|
| **Decreases** | **88%** |
| Increases | 8% |
| Doesn't change | 4% |

**Key reasoning:**

- **Mar 16 value = 60**, already down 40% from the Mar 15 peak of 100. The spike was driven by a specific viral conspiracy (AI video showing Netanyahu with "6 fingers"), which has been definitively debunked by Netanyahu himself with a coffee shop video and by Snopes fact-checking.
- **Historical pattern**: The previous spike in this data (Mar 1-3, peaked at 13) returned to baseline (~1) in 3-4 days. Post-spike decay is the dominant dynamic.
- **"Doesn't change" requires landing in [57, 63]** — an extremely narrow 7-point band. For a volatile search term starting at a spike peak, this is near-impossible 8 days later.
- **War context provides upside risk**: The active Israel-Iran war and IRGC threats could trigger new death rumors, but "Increases" requires exceeding 63 on Mar 24 — essentially requiring a new event even more dramatic than the debunked conspiracy peak.
- **Monte Carlo simulations** (100k runs each) confirm: blended estimate across conservative and aggressive models gives ~89% Decreases.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will the interest in “netanyahu dead” change between 2026-03-16 and 2026-03-24 according to Google Trends?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42689)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves “Doesn't change” if the value on the timeline at [https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=netanyahu%20dead&date=2026-02-22%202026-03-24](https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=netanyahu%20dead&date=2026-02-22%202026-03-24) for 2026-03-24 is within 3 of the value at 2026-03-16. It resolves “Increases” if the value is more than 3 greater, and resolves “Decreases” if the value is more than 3 lower.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will access the data on Google Trends using [SerpApi](https://serpapi.com/), and compare the height of the timeline for the two aforementioned dates. The specific python query will be structured as follows:
```
from serpapi import GoogleSearch

search = GoogleSearch(params={
  "api_key": API_KEY,
  "engine": "google_trends",
  "data_type": "TIMESERIES",
  "geo": "US",
  "tz": 0,
  "q": "netanyahu dead",
  "date": "2026-02-22 2026-03-24",
})
search.get_dict()
```
Note that there may be differences between the results returned by the API and the data appearing on the page. This seems to be due to the 'tz' parameter not having the intended effect in-browser. In this case, the API results will be considered authoritative.
Note that the precision of the timeline will be 1 day, so this will compare the overall interest for the whole day as determined by Google Trends.
Dates are determined in UTC.
If the data is no longer available, or the script fails, this question will be annulled or manually resolved by a moderator.
